@@ -89,7 +89,17 @@ Current main already handles this request: non-native OpenAI Responses endpoints
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing as implemented on `main` after Codex review. Current OpenClaw already strips `prompt_cache_key` and `prompt_cache_retention` for proxy-like / non-native `openai-responses` routes (`src/agents/provider-attribution.ts:640`, `src/agents/openai-responses-payload-policy.ts:148`), and regression coverage exists for the exact keep-vs-strip behavior (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner-extraparams.test.ts:3354`). There is also an explicit model compat override at `models.providers.*.models.*.compat.supportsPromptCacheKey` (`src/config/types.models.ts:57`). The shipped changelog entry for the automatic strip is under `2026.3.22` (`CHANGELOG.md:2156`, `CHANGELOG.md:2275`). If vLLM still returns 500s on a current build, that now looks like a different request-shape incompatibility rather than missing `prompt_cache_key` opt-out, so please open a fresh issue with current repro steps, config, and payload/log details.
+Closing as implemented on `main` after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a).
+
+Current main already handles this request: non-native OpenAI Responses endpoints strip `prompt_cache_key`/`prompt_cache_retention` automatically, and model compat now exposes `supportsPromptCacheKey` for explicit override. The changelog shows the automatic strip shipped in `2026.3.22` and the compat override landed later in unreleased notes.
+
+Evidence:
+- **Proxy-like Responses endpoints now default to stripping prompt cache fields:** `resolveProviderRequestCapabilities` sets `shouldStripResponsesPromptCache` for Responses APIs on explicit proxy-like endpoints, with `compat.supportsPromptCacheKey` as an override. (`src/agents/provider-attribution.ts:640`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Payload policy deletes prompt cache fields when stripping is enabled:** `applyOpenAIResponsesPayloadPolicy` removes both `prompt_cache_key` and `prompt_cache_retention` when the resolved policy says to strip prompt cache. (`src/agents/openai-responses-payload-policy.ts:148`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Regression tests cover the exact requested behavior:** Tests assert prompt cache fields are removed for non-OpenAI `openai-responses` endpoints and preserved for direct OpenAI/Azure OpenAI endpoints. (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner-extraparams.test.ts:3354`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Explicit model-level compat override exists:** `ModelCompatConfig` includes `supportsPromptCacheKey?: boolean`, which is the current config surface for forcing keep/strip behavior per model. (`src/config/types.models.ts:57`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Changelog records both the shipped fix and later compat option:** `2026.3.22` notes the automatic stripping fix for non-OpenAI-compatible Responses endpoints, and unreleased notes add `models.providers.*.models.*.compat.supportsPromptCacheKey`. (`CHANGELOG.md:2156`, `cec34821754a`)
+
 Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Fix evidence: release [2026.3.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/2026.3.22).

The latest release still contains this implementation, so this issue no longer tracks an open gap. +Closing as implemented on `main` after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a). + +Current main already renders safe inline markdown images in Control UI assistant replies, with tests and release notes showing it shipped in version 2026.2.13 and later security hardening preserved inline `data:image/...` rendering. + +Evidence: +- **Assistant reply markdown goes through the sanitized markdown renderer:** Control UI chat bubbles render assistant/tool markdown via `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml(markdown)`, so markdown image support applies to agent replies. (`ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts:1477`, `cec34821754a`) +- **Markdown renderer emits inline image tags for safe markdown images:** `md.renderer.rules.image` returns `` for `data:image/...;base64,...` sources and flattens other URLs to alt text. (`ui/src/ui/markdown.ts:409`, `cec34821754a`) +- **Regression test covers inline markdown image rendering:** The markdown test suite asserts that `![Chart](data:image/png;base64,...)` produces an `` with the `markdown-inline-image` class. @@ -93,7 +93,18 @@ Close as `clawhub`: the request is coherent and useful, but current `main` posit
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing as `clawhub` after a Codex review of current `main` (`cec3482`). This is a useful idea, but it fits OpenClaw's extension surfaces better than core. `VISION.md:52` says core stays lean and optional capability should usually ship as plugins. The needed integration points already exist: `docs/automation/hooks.md:34` documents `agent:bootstrap` and `gateway:startup`, `src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts:7` passes the workspace bootstrap files into that hook path, and `src/hooks/internal-hook-types.ts:3` gives hooks a `messages` array for user-visible alerts. For periodic verification, `docs/automation/cron-jobs.md:10` already provides Gateway-native scheduling and chat/webhook delivery. Since the request is specifically about operator-owned workspace files like `AGENTS.md` and `SOUL.md` (`docs/concepts/agent-workspace.md:62`), this is better handled as a ClawHub plugin or hook pack than as OpenClaw core.
+Closing as better suited for ClawHub/community plugin work after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a).
+
+Close as `clawhub`: the request is coherent and useful, but current `main` positions this kind of workspace-file integrity monitoring as extension/plugin territory rather than OpenClaw core.
+
+Evidence:
+- **Vision scopes optional capability to plugins:** The project vision says core should stay lean and that optional capability should usually ship as plugins, with a high bar for adding optional plugins to core. (`VISION.md:52`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Hooks already cover bootstrap and startup lifecycle:** Internal hooks already expose `agent:bootstrap` and `gateway:startup`, which are the exact lifecycle points the issue asks for when hashing persona/bootstrap files at startup and during agent bootstrap. (`docs/automation/hooks.md:34`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Hook events can emit user-visible alerts:** Hook handlers can push strings onto `event.messages`, and the internal hook event type defines `messages` specifically as messages sent back to the user. That matches the issue's proposed 'my brain was just replaced' alert behavior. (`src/hooks/internal-hook-types.ts:3`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Bootstrap hooks receive the workspace bootstrap file set:** `applyBootstrapHookOverrides` passes `workspaceDir` and mutable `bootstrapFiles` through the `agent:bootstrap` event, so a hook/plugin can inspect or hash `AGENTS.md`/`SOUL.md`/`USER.md`-class files without core changes. (`src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts:7`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Periodic checks already have a built-in scheduler:** Cron is a built-in scheduler that runs inside the Gateway and can deliver output to chat channels or webhooks, so recurring integrity verification does not need a new core scheduler surface. (`docs/automation/cron-jobs.md:10`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **The monitored files are operator-owned workspace content:** The agent workspace docs define `AGENTS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `USER.md`, and `IDENTITY.md` as private workspace files, and recommend backing that workspace with a private git repo. That makes this more of an operator-side integrity workflow than a core runtime contract. (`docs/concepts/agent-workspace.md:9`, `cec34821754a`)
+
 Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Specific fixed release/commit was not determined by this review.

@@ -87,7 +87,17 @@ Current main already routes subagent/sessions_spawn result messages through the
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing this as implemented on `main` after Codex review. On current `main` (`cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2`), tool-tagged messages are normalized to `toolResult` in `ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.ts:239`, and the chat renderer displays those messages in the collapsible Tool Output UI in `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts:1334` instead of a normal user bubble. There is direct `sessions_spawn` coverage in `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts:459` asserting `Tool output` rendering for a result payload with `childSessionKey`, and the subagent completion path in `src/agents/subagent-announce-output.ts:152` plus `src/agents/subagent-announce.format.e2e.test.ts:1868` now prefers tool-result output while explicitly ignoring user text. I could not determine the first release tag that shipped this from the available local history, so I’m leaving the release field unset.
+Closing as implemented on `main` after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a).
+
+Current main already routes subagent/sessions_spawn result messages through the tool-result path and renders them as collapsible Tool output, not as plain user chat bubbles.
+
+Evidence:
+- **Tool-tagged messages normalize away from user role:** `normalizeMessage()` promotes any message carrying `toolCallId`, tool content blocks, or a tool name to `toolResult`, so the UI no longer relies on the original raw role for these payloads. (`ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.ts:239`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Renderer shows tool output UI:** `renderGroupedMessage()` treats `toolResult`/`tool_result` and any message with `toolCallId` as a tool message, labels it `Tool output`, and renders it in the collapsible tool-message container rather than a normal user bubble. (`ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts:1334`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Direct sessions_spawn UI coverage:** UI test coverage explicitly renders a `sessions_spawn` result containing `childSessionKey` and asserts the transcript shows `Tool output` plus the structured result content, including the collapsible behavior. (`ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts:459`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Subagent completion flow prefers tool output and ignores user text:** Subagent announce output extraction reads `toolResult`/`tool` messages from chat history, selects that output when assistant text is empty, and the e2e coverage verifies it falls back to tool output while explicitly not announcing user text. (`src/agents/subagent-announce-output.ts:152`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Worktree stayed clean:** Read-only inspection left the checkout unchanged. (`cec34821754a`)
+
 Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Fix evidence: commit [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2).

@@ -91,7 +91,18 @@ Current `main` already implements the requested hardening bundle, though not alw
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing as implemented on main after a Codex review of the current tree and the shipped `v2026.4.22` release. The four requested hardening areas are already present: the Control UI uses a bundled local logo asset (`ui/src/ui/views/agents-utils.ts:243`, `ui/public/favicon.svg:1`), gateway HTTP handling applies baseline security headers (`src/gateway/server-http.ts:900`, `src/gateway/http-common.ts:15`), Control UI responses add `X-Frame-Options` and CSP on top (`src/gateway/control-ui.ts:142`), CI now enforces actionlint and zizmor (`.github/workflows/workflow-sanity.yml:52`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml:367`), and repo security scanning includes both CodeQL and dependency auditing (`.github/workflows/codeql.yml:1`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml:394`). The exact workflow split differs from the original proposal, but the requested hardening is already in place.
+Closing as implemented on `main` after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a).
+
+Current `main` already implements the requested hardening bundle, though not always with the exact workflow/file split proposed in the issue. The Control UI logo is local, gateway responses now get baseline security headers with stricter Control UI headers on top, and CI already enforces actionlint/zizmor plus CodeQL and dependency auditing.
+
+Evidence:
+- **Local Control UI asset:** `agentLogoUrl` now resolves to the bundled `favicon.svg`, and the asset exists in-repo. (`ui/src/ui/views/agents-utils.ts:243`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Baseline gateway security headers:** `server-http` applies `setDefaultSecurityHeaders` to HTTP requests, and that helper sets `X-Content-Type-Options`, `Referrer-Policy`, and `Permissions-Policy`. (`src/gateway/server-http.ts:900`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Control UI hardening headers:** Control UI responses additionally set `X-Frame-Options: DENY` and a CSP, with tests asserting those headers. (`src/gateway/control-ui.ts:142`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **CI enforces actionlint and zizmor:** `workflow-sanity.yml` runs actionlint, and `ci.yml` audits changed workflows with zizmor. (`.github/workflows/workflow-sanity.yml:52`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Dependency/security scanning is present:** A dedicated CodeQL workflow exists, and main CI also runs production dependency auditing. (`.github/workflows/codeql.yml:1`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Shipped release proof:** The same hardening paths are present in release tag `v2026.4.22`, so this is not just unreleased `main` state. (`00bd2cf7a376`)
+
 Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).

@@ -89,7 +89,17 @@ Current main already implements progressive, context-aware skill disclosure with
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing this as implemented based on a Codex review of current `main` and the shipped `v2026.4.22` tag. OpenClaw now handles this through progressive skill disclosure rather than trigger frontmatter: the system prompt tells the agent to scan `<available_skills>` descriptions and read only the matching `SKILL.md` when needed (`src/agents/system-prompt.ts:162-167`), and the prompt catalog itself contains only each skill's name, description, and location (`src/agents/skills/skill-contract.ts:44-60`). The catalog is also compacted/truncated under budget pressure (`src/agents/skills/workspace.ts:791-807`; `docs/tools/skills.md:360-376`), and session snapshots can refresh when the watcher or effective skill allowlist changes (`docs/tools/skills.md:331-347`; `src/agents/agent-command.ts:575-607`). That means the original token-cost problem this issue was tracking is already addressed on main, so there is no separate remaining core feature gap to keep open here.
+Closing as implemented on `main` after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a).
+
+Current main already implements progressive, context-aware skill disclosure with bounded prompt cost: the model scans a lightweight `available_skills` catalog and reads only the matching `SKILL.md` when needed, with compact/truncated catalog fallback and mid-session snapshot refreshes for watcher/allowlist changes. That addresses the token-cost request even though the shipped design differs from the issue's trigger-frontmatter sketch.
+
+Evidence:
+- **System prompt uses lazy skill loading:** The agent is instructed to scan `available_skills`, then read exactly one matching `SKILL.md` only after selection; if none apply it reads none. (`src/agents/system-prompt.ts:162`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Prompt contains only skill catalog metadata, not full skill bodies:** `formatSkillsForPrompt` injects only each skill's name, description, and location into the prompt catalog. (`src/agents/skills/skill-contract.ts:44`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Prompt budget compacts and truncates the catalog:** When the full catalog is too large, OpenClaw switches to compact formatting or truncates the list with an explicit warning, directly addressing token pressure. (`src/agents/skills/workspace.ts:791`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Docs quantify low deterministic token overhead:** The skills docs describe the session snapshot model and document that the prompt cost is a compact XML catalog with 195 chars base overhead plus 97 chars per skill plus field lengths, not full skill text. (`docs/tools/skills.md:331`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Snapshot refreshes when eligibility changes:** Current main rebuilds the session skill snapshot when the snapshot version changes or the effective agent skill filter changes, and the docs also describe watcher-driven mid-session hot reload. (`src/agents/agent-command.ts:575`, `cec34821754a`)
+
 Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Fix evidence: commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).

@@ -87,7 +87,18 @@ Current `main` already implements agent-driven model switching well enough: agen
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing as implemented on `main` after Codex review. Current `main` already gives agents a first-class way to switch models from normal conversation: `session_status` accepts `model` and persists the session override in `src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts:473`, the agent prompt explicitly advertises that per-session override in `src/agents/system-prompt.ts:507`, and the same prompt tells agents to use first-class tools instead of equivalent slash/CLI commands in `src/agents/system-prompt.ts:728`. The runtime and tests also cover live-switch handoff for active runs (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts:879`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts:778`). I could not confirm a tagged release containing this yet, so `fixedRelease` is left null.
+Closing as implemented on `main` after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a).
+
+Current `main` already implements agent-driven model switching well enough: agents have a first-class `session_status(model=...)` tool, the system prompt explicitly advertises that override and tells agents to use tools instead of equivalent slash/CLI commands, and the runtime/test coverage handles live-switch retries for active runs.
+
+Evidence:
+- **`session_status` accepts and applies model overrides:** The tool schema includes an optional `model` field, and the execute path resolves the requested model, applies it to the session entry, persists it, and marks `liveModelSwitchPending` for runtime handoff. (`src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts:473`, `1daa552d5f42`)
+- **Agent prompt advertises model override on `session_status`:** The injected tool summary tells the agent that `session_status` can answer model-state questions and set an optional per-session model override. (`src/agents/system-prompt.ts:507`, `1daa552d5f42`)
+- **Agent prompt prefers first-class tools over slash commands:** The prompt explicitly instructs agents to use a first-class tool directly instead of asking the user to run an equivalent CLI or slash command. (`src/agents/system-prompt.ts:728`, `1daa552d5f42`)
+- **Runtime supports deferred live model switches:** During an active attempt, a pending live switch is detected and retried via `LiveSessionModelSwitchError` when restart is safe. (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts:879`, `1daa552d5f42`)
+- **Tests cover override persistence and live switch behavior:** Tests verify `session_status` can set a model override on the current session and that live switches are queued for active runs. (`src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts:778`, `1daa552d5f42`)
+- **Docs describe the agent-facing override surface:** The session tools docs now describe `session_status` as a `/status`-style tool that can set a per-session model override and clear it with `model=default`. (`docs/concepts/session-tool.md:81`, `1daa552d5f42`)
+
 Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Fix evidence: commit [1daa552d5f42](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc).

@@ -97,7 +97,18 @@ Current `main` already covers the request: the Control UI supports editing cron
 
 ## Close Comment

 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing from a Codex review because current `main` already has this capability, even though it does not use the exact YAML shape proposed here. Host `exec` is already gated by built-in exec approvals, allowlists, and ask-fallback policy (`docs/tools/exec-approvals.md`, `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-shared.ts`), and generic tool interception now exists through plugin `before_tool_call` hooks that can either block a tool call or require human approval (`src/plugins/hook-types.ts`, `src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.ts`). The approval UX is documented for the exec overlay, Telegram/Discord interactions, and `/approve`, and the behavior is covered by end-to-end tests for allow, deny, timeout, and fail-closed cases (`docs/plugins/building-plugins.md`, `src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts`). I also confirmed these surfaces are already present in release `v2026.4.22`.
+Closing as implemented on `main` after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a).
+
+Current main already provides the requested safety model: host `exec` is guarded by built-in exec approvals/allowlists/ask-fallback, and plugins can intercept any tool call with `before_tool_call` to either block it or require human approval. The exact YAML sketch from the issue was not added, but the underlying capability is present and shipped.
+
+Evidence:
+- **Built-in exec approvals guard host command execution:** The docs describe exec approvals as a host guardrail where commands run only when policy, allowlist, and optional user approval all agree; the effective policy is the stricter of `tools.exec.*` and `~/.openclaw/exec-approvals.json`, with deny fallback when no UI is available. (`docs/tools/exec-approvals.md:10`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Runtime enforces stricter host exec policy:** `resolveExecHostApprovalContext` merges requested exec policy with host approvals and throws when the effective host security resolves to `deny`, so host config can tighten and block execution. (`src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-shared.ts:198`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Plugin hook contract supports blocking or approval-gating tool calls:** `PluginHookBeforeToolCallResult` includes both `block`/`blockReason` and `requireApproval` fields with approval metadata, which is the generic interception seam for tool safety policies. (`src/plugins/hook-types.ts:324`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Agent loop pauses tool execution for human approval:** When a `before_tool_call` hook returns `requireApproval`, the agent requests `plugin.approval.request`, waits on `plugin.approval.waitDecision`, allows on `allow-once`/`allow-always`, blocks on `deny`, and blocks or allows on timeout according to the configured timeout behavior. (`src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.ts:219`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Gateway exposes plugin approval flow for human-in-the-loop resolution:** The gateway implements `plugin.approval.request/list/waitDecision/resolve`, creates approval records, and forwards approval requests for delivery to approval clients. (`src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.ts:26`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Docs describe approval UX across channels:** Plugin docs state that `before_tool_call: { requireApproval: true }` pauses execution and prompts through the exec approval overlay, Telegram buttons, Discord interactions, or `/approve` on any channel. (`docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:184`, `cec34821754a`)
+

 ## Close Comment
 
-Codex review: this looks like a better fit for the community plugin/ClawHub path than for OpenClaw core. Current main explicitly says optional capability should usually ship as plugins and be maintained outside the repo (`VISION.md:54`), and the roadmap guardrails call out commercial integrations and wrapper channels as out of scope for core by default (`VISION.md:101`). The repo already documents how to build channel plugins (`docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md:11`), treats community plugins/ClawHub as the canonical discovery path (`docs/plugins/community.md:9`), and even showcases an external Vapi bridge today (`docs/start/showcase.md:282`). Closing this as a scope-fit item rather than a missing core feature; if someone wants to pursue Vapi support, the right path is an external OpenClaw plugin/bridge published through the community plugin flow.
+Closing as better suited for ClawHub/community plugin work after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a).
+
+The request is a real idea, but current `main` treats this as community-plugin territory rather than an OpenClaw core cleanup item: new channel integrations are documented as plugins, community/ClawHub is the canonical distribution path, and the docs already point to an external Vapi bridge.
+
+Evidence:
+- **Vision keeps optional integrations out of core:** Current main says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, preferably hosted and maintained outside this repo, with a high bar for adding optional plugins to core. (`VISION.md:54`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Vision scope guardrails match this request:** The roadmap guardrails explicitly list commercial service integrations and wrapper channels as things OpenClaw will not merge for now unless there is unusually strong rationale. (`VISION.md:101`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **New channels are expected to be plugins:** The channel-plugin guide says a new messaging platform should be built as a ChannelPlugin, with core keeping the shared message tool while the plugin owns config, security, pairing, outbound delivery, and threading. (`docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md:11`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Community/ClawHub is the canonical path:** Current docs say community plugins can extend OpenClaw with channels and that ClawHub is the canonical discovery surface for community plugins. (`docs/plugins/community.md:9`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Repo already points to an external Vapi bridge:** The showcase on current main includes 'Clawdia phone bridge', described as a 'Vapi voice assistant to OpenClaw HTTP bridge' for near real-time phone calls. (`docs/start/showcase.md:282`, `cec34821754a`)
+- **Telephony already lives in plugin land:** Voice calling is documented as an installable plugin rather than a core channel, reinforcing that call transports belong in plugins. (`docs/plugins/voice-call.md:9`, `cec34821754a`)
+
